mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with federal funds rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power and deteriorating corporate earnings expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which depresses corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.1 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing contraction and supply-side bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines below its 12-month average are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, breaking the negative predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real purchasing power and force central banks to tighten policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Monthly CPIAUCSL increases show zero or negative correlation with subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 24-month rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent on an annualized basis, aggregate demand contraction becomes severe enough to trigger earnings downgrades across the S&P 500, resulting in negative forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth remains below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration and hidden slack, which undermines economic resilience and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a positive 20-day return in over 65 percent of triggered months, negating the hypothesized negative mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, a sudden pause in federal funds rate hikes signals policy normalization and relieves equity valuation pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Federal funds rate pauses during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances, invalidating the positive signal.
